name: Kerri
title: Owner & Founder
bio:
Kerri stepped into her first Bikram Yoga class in Truckee, CA in 2003 after beating up her body with years of sports and distance running and suffering from chronic pain & anxiety. It was the hardest & most painful thing she had ever tried but immediately felt the physical, mental & emotional benefits and knew this was something she had to pursue.

After returning home from a deployment with the military, she made the decision to change her course and attended the demanding Bikram teacher training in 2005, kids yoga training in 2006, Core Power Yoga in 2009 and Inferno Hot Pilates in 2022.

Kerri brought hot yoga to Sandpoint in 2008 & had to close the doors early in 2011 due to personal/family issues. The incredible support of the community & amazing teachers encouraged her to bring it back in 2016. She is grateful to live a life pursuing her love of teaching & all things mother nature.

name: Allie
title: Yoga & Fitness Instructor
bio:
Allie grew up on the rolling hills of the Palouse in an athletic and outdoorsy family. Her childhood was full of horseback riding, mountain climbing, triathlons, and rafting. She watched her mother practice and later teach yoga, and she fell in step.

Allie has attended three yoga trainings, including additional training for pre/postnatal. She began teaching fitness classes at the U of I when she was 18. She taught at the University for 5 years and was an instructor at NIAC for 10 years. Allie believes that yoga is more than just a workout; it's a journey. She encourages others to make their practice personal, to start where they are today, challenge their preconceived limits, and enjoy the process.

When not at the studio, Allie can be found on the trails, in the lake, or behind a camera.

name: Amanda
title: Hot Pilates Instructor
bio:
Two years ago, Amanda moved to Sandpoint and quickly found herself at home in the SHY community. With a strong background in fitness — holding her CFI 1 and spending nine years training CrossFit — she's excited to bring her passion for strength and mindful movement into the studio, teaching Hot Pilates each week.

Outside of the studio, Amanda is a devoted mama to two boys and loves spending her spare time exploring the beauty of North Idaho, whether hiking, adventuring, or simply soaking in the outdoors. She also runs her own business, Luna Alchemy Co., where she shares her love of sound healing.

Amanda continues to embrace balance, peace, and connection, and she's grateful to share that energy with the SHY community.

name: Canyon
title: Inferno Hot Pilates Instructor
bio:
Five years ago, Canyon made the life-changing decision to trade the bustling cityscape for the serene beauty of North Idaho and has never looked back. Living in the charming region of the Sandpoint area, he's had the privilege of immersing himself in Idaho's breathtaking outdoors.

When he's not soaking up the natural wonders of Idaho, you'll often find Canyon in the studio guiding enthusiasts through the transformative world of Inferno Hot Pilates. As a certified instructor, he's passionate about helping individuals achieve their fitness goals while igniting their inner strength.

name: Chelsey
title: Vinyasa Instructor
bio:
Chelsey has been teaching all-levels vinyasa for over a decade. She was introduced to heated Vinyasa Yoga in 2011, following a suggestion from her meditation therapist. It was love at first sweat. She quickly connected with the practice's ability to link breath to movement and the inner peace yoga brought her.

Forever a student, Chelsey has over 500 hours of yoga education, from 200hr and 300hr teacher trainings, to continuing education with world renowned teachers like Jason Crandell. She is constantly inspired by each and every teacher she has the privilege of learning from, whether that be a regular class or a continuing education format.

You can expect Chelsey's classes to be creative, strength building, and purposeful. Each class is intentionally designed to focus on specific areas of the physical body while incorporating a touch of spirituality. With an emphasis on awareness, breath, and a little bit of mantra, she challenges students through a progressive and intelligent flow. Prepare to sweat a lot, laugh a little, and perhaps even find an emotional connection to the rhythm of the music.

After living in Fort Collins, CO, for over 12 years, Chelsey sought a slower-paced lifestyle that would allow her to enjoy the outdoors even more. This search led her to Sandpoint in March 2020. When Chelsey isn't in the studio, she is outside with her husband and dog, gardening, creating art, and/or traveling.

name: Cheri
title: Yoga & Inferno Hot Pilates Instructor
bio:
It's a wonder sometimes how we end up where we are. Cheri's first experience on a yoga mat when she was 23 instantly hooked her into the practice. She considered yoga teacher training back then, but her path meandered in other directions.

Fast forward a couple decades, having tried her hand in restaurant work, accepting commissions and selling her artwork, being a couples counselor, art therapist, chemical dependency counselor, past life regressionist, art teacher, and a stay-at-home mom (best job in the world) — all the while returning time and again to her yoga mat and meditation practice — Cheri can now be found teaching Inferno Hot Pilates and yoga.

Her passion is to help people awaken to their inherent power to consciously create a life of joy, strength, and beauty. Both yoga and hot Pilates allow us to see how our thoughts shape our reality, how our bodies are amazing tools of exploration and insight and wisdom, and help us to align our mind, body, and spirit.

When not in the hot studio, Cheri can be found spending time with her family, home schooling her daughter, enjoying the beauty of nature, and fueling her creative passion in her art studio.

name: Ellie
title: Power Vinyasa Instructor
bio:
Ellie took her first yoga class here at SHY with Kerri and Julie at the age of 16. Being a competitive athlete throughout high school and college, Ellie fell in and out of love with her self practice. It wasn't until after college did she truly understand her relationship with it and began to cultivate a consistent practice.

Ellie got her Vinyasa RYT200 certification from Santosha Yoga Institute in Nusa Lembogan, Bali. Her style is based off her athletic past, challenging the individual to work through the sensations linking their breath with movement in a fast paced power vinyasa.

When Ellie is not on the mat, you can find her roaming the mountains and rivers...

name: Jenn
title: Yoga Instructor
bio:
Born and raised in small town Idaho, Jenn discovered yoga in 2000, when she moved out of state to the big city. She fell deeply in love with yoga immediately, and after decades of daily practice, credits yoga with saving her life.

Jenn's yoga is influenced both by her decades of personal practice and by the extensive training in multiple modalities she has undergone in her years as a yoga instructor. She believes that yoga is so much more than the physical poses — it is a golden thread that leads to self-realization, divine connection, and peace in our own skin.

Jenn is overjoyed to be back in her beloved Idaho, and spends much of her time out enjoying its beauty with her intrepid pug, Billie Jean.

name: Jennings
title: Hot Yoga Instructor
bio:
Jennings is an artist, entrepreneur, outdoor lover, and self-proclaimed recovering project junkie whose mind is always dreaming up the next idea. She found Bikram in college in Southern California and the practice became her medicine — discipline, heat, truth, and clarity.

After moving to Idaho with no place to practice, she waited until Sandpoint Hot Yoga opened its doors in 2007. Jennings trained with Bikram in 2009 and the practice has been woven into her life ever since.

As a busy mom of two, she's deeply grateful for the challenge, the stillness, and the mind-clearing magic that happens in the hot room.

name: Julie
title: Bikram & Vinyasa Instructor
bio:
Julie's yoga journey began right here at Sandpoint Hot Yoga with Kerri. She was seeking an activity that would help heal her body from years of strenuous activity and poor eating habits. Yoga not only healed her body but gave her a sense of peace she had never known.

Julie completed an RYT yoga teacher training at Yoga Tribe in Huntington Beach, CA where she learned to blend Bikram & Vinyasa. Julie enjoys teaching all levels and watching the transformation of her students' bodies, minds, and souls.

When not in the yoga studio, Julie is constantly playing in the mountains on her bike or skis. She believes yoga is what keeps her body young to participate in all her adventures.

name: Megan
title: Yoga, Pilates & Barre Instructor
bio:
Megan's family moved to Sandpoint from CDA when she was 8 years old to escape city life. She grew up 30 min from town at the end of Bottle Bay on 21 acres spending her childhood building forts, bike riding and running through the forest.

Megan started running competitively as a young teenager, and soon after, developed a love for weight training. She started joining group exercise classes and then developed a love for yoga and pilates. Meg earned her ACE certification in group exercise in 2020 and has since continued her education to earn certification in many formats. Over the last 5 years she has taught barre, pilates, yoga, interval HIIT formats, strength formats, and indoor cycling. She loves everything fitness. She enjoys the variety and believes variety is key to a strong, balanced, healthy body and mind. She has made wonderful friendships during this journey, has overcome insecurities, and discovered a love for teaching.

She is a very happy wife and mother of two amazing boys. They spend their time at the soccer field, on Schweitzer, hiking, boating, camping, and chasing the sun to Mexico yearly.

name: Noelle
title: Yoga Instructor
bio:
Noelle was first introduced to yoga as a way to remain healthy and comfortable while pregnant with her twin boys. Breathwork, increased strength and flexibility, and healing of her lower back are what initially inspired her to attend a RYA 200hr teacher training in Spokane in 2010.

Since then, Noelle has journeyed into a wide array of studies and teaching incorporating Western science/medicine, Chinese medicine, somatic work, functional movement, as well as the inspiration of other yoga teachers. Movement, yin and restorative, spiritual teachings, dreamwork, meditation, and Yoga Nidra are each a part of her own practice, and she feels that she can authentically share what is a part of her practice on and off the mat.

Noelle loves guiding people to dive into this world to explore the benefits in their own body and this is reflected in her wide array of knowledge and diverse teaching styles.

name: Riane
title: Inferno Hot Pilates Instructor
bio:
Growing up on the east coast, Riane had an innate love for the ocean but after graduating nursing school in Virginia she felt a longing for the mountains. After travel nursing the country for 5 years, and 7 states later, she found a home in North Idaho where her love for the water and the mountains merged.

Once a gymnast, Riane found herself venturing into various avenues of sports. She found strength through all different modalities of exercise. She is inspired by the dedication and resilience it takes to show up and fuel your body in whatever format you align with.

When she isn't teaching her new love for inferno pilates you can find her outside on the lake and rivers, hiking, exploring new places, and listening to live music.

name: Suzanne
title: Vinyasa Instructor
bio:
Suzanne was first introduced to yoga in 1982 for college credit at Sonoma State University. She incorporated some of her yoga knowledge and asanas while teaching aerobics years later at SWAC in Sandpoint but it wasn't until she went back to University in Boulder, CO in 2009 that she “rediscovered” yoga. Since then, she has been a regular practitioner, first to supplement her athletic activities, and later discovered the beauty of what yoga does for her inner life.

She had wanted to get her yoga certification for quite some time but didn't feel she had the focus until the fall of 2022 when she began her 500 hour teacher certification. She completed her training in the Spring of 2023 and since then, Suzanne has brought her unique vinyasa style and a wealth of knowledge and life experience to Sandpoint Hot Yoga.

Some of Suzanne's favorite things about teaching include getting to know this amazing community, guiding yogis towards a deeper understanding, and the feeling when the room is in collective breath. Suzanne's other passions outside of the warm room include snowboarding/snowshoeing, horses, dogs, the ocean and sunsets.

name: Toshi
title: Power Vinyasa Instructor
bio:
TWISM
This World IS Mine . . .
We can not control the unpredictable nature of the world AROUND us, but we can control our reactions and the world WITHIN us. Our attitudes and our behaviors are our choice, so we choose to be light.

I'm Toshi! I love live music, chocolate chip cookies, walking my dog, skiing, and so many other things. What makes you happy? What elevates your joy? Bring those answers along with your smile to one of my classes.

My stylings borrow from Baptiste Power Yoga, Traditional Vinyasa and Hatha with subtle infusions of the 8-limb path. My best hope is that you leave feeling lifted physically as well as spiritually.

My first certification was a 50 hour NHA certification for strength training through CorePower Yoga in 2014. That allowed me to teach their “sculpt” template. Later that year I earned my 200 hour RYT certification.
